vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:deft-directive-setup
vBRIEFInfo.description: MVP scope vBRIEF for cursorSpeedometer — GPS trip computer for riders

narratives.Overview: cursorSpeedometer is a native iOS trip-computer app for motorcycle, bicycle, and e-bike riders. It displays GPS-derived current speed, odometer, and standard trip stats (max speed, distance traveled, average speed) in a large, glanceable UI optimized for use while riding.
narratives.ProblemStatement: Riders need a simple, reliable speedometer and trip computer on their phone without map clutter or complex setup. Existing navigation apps are map-centric and distracting; dedicated hardware is expensive.
narratives.Goals:
1. Show live GPS speed with sub-second updates while riding.
2. Track trip distance, max speed, and average speed for the current session.
3. Provide a persistent odometer across trips.
4. Support both imperial and metric units (user-selectable).
5. Keep the UI readable at a glance with minimal interaction while moving.
6. Let riders choose display color themes: Day, Night (all-red), and Amber.
7. Auto-switch to Night theme at sunset and Day theme at sunrise (user can disable).
8. Adjust display brightness with ambient conditions — ideally tracking ambient light, with a sunrise/sunset fallback schedule.
narratives.NonGoals: Turn-by-turn navigation, route planning, social features, Apple Watch companion, cloud sync, or map display in MVP.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: MUST display current speed updated from CoreLocation GPS at least once per second when location accuracy is ≤ 50 m.
FR-2: MUST display trip distance for the active session, resetting when the user taps Reset Trip.
FR-3: MUST display max speed and average speed for the active session.
FR-4: MUST display a cumulative odometer that persists across app launches.
FR-5: MUST allow the user to switch between mph/miles and km/h/km.
FR-6: MUST request When-In-Use location permission on first launch and degrade gracefully when denied.
FR-7: SHOULD offer a Ride Mode that keeps the screen awake while a trip is active.
FR-8: SHOULD filter GPS jitter below 3 km/h when stationary.
FR-9: MUST let the user select from three preset display color themes: Day (high-contrast default for daylight), Night (all-red monochrome for night-vision preservation), and Amber (warm amber/yellow tones on dark background for low-light and dusk riding). Selected theme MUST apply immediately to all ride-screen elements (speed, labels, stat tiles, backgrounds).
FR-10: MUST persist the user's theme choice and auto-theme preference across app launches. When auto-theme is enabled, the stored manual theme selection is remembered but overridden by the solar schedule until the user disables auto-theme or picks a fixed theme.
FR-11: MUST adjust ride-screen brightness automatically. Preferred: track device ambient light via UIScreen brightness + optional UIScreen.main.traitCollection / ambient sensor polling to dim in low light and brighten in daylight. Minimum fallback: schedule-based dimming at local sunset and brightening at local sunrise (computed from device location/timezone).
FR-12: MUST allow the user to override auto-brightness with a manual brightness slider in Settings; manual override persists until the user re-enables auto-brightness.
FR-13: MUST auto-switch display theme on a solar schedule when auto-theme is enabled (default: on): switch to Night at local sunset, switch to Day at local sunrise. Schedule computed from device location and timezone (same solar calc as brightness fallback).
FR-14: MUST allow the user to disable auto-theme in Settings and manually pin any preset (Day, Night, or Amber); manual pin persists until auto-theme is re-enabled.
narratives.NonFunctionalRequirements:
NFR-1: UI MUST remain legible in direct sunlight (Day theme: high-contrast, large typography), in darkness (Night theme: no bloom or glare), and at dusk (Amber theme: warm tones readable without washing out).
NFR-2: Location updates MUST NOT drain battery excessively — use distance filter and appropriate accuracy for cycling/motorcycle speeds.
NFR-3: App MUST target iOS 16+ and run on iPhone only for MVP.
NFR-4: Core trip-computer logic MUST be unit-testable without a live GPS fix.
NFR-5: MUST achieve ≥ 85% test coverage on testable Swift modules.
NFR-6: Brightness and theme transitions MUST feel smooth (no jarring step changes); throttle updates to ≤ 1 Hz.
NFR-7: Night (all-red) theme MUST use only red-channel hues (#FF0000 family) on a near-black background to preserve rider night vision.
NFR-8: Amber theme MUST use warm amber/yellow hues (#FFBF00–#FFA500 family) on a dark background; no blue or white channel dominance.
narratives.Architecture:
SwiftUI app with MVVM-ish separation:
- LocationService: wraps CLLocationManager, publishes speed/distance updates.
- TripComputerEngine: pure Swift — computes max/avg speed, trip distance, odometer from location samples (fully unit-tested).
- TripStore: persists odometer, unit preference, display theme, auto-theme flag, pinned theme, and brightness settings via UserDefaults.
- DisplayTheme: enum + color palette (Day, Night/all-red, Amber); injected via SwiftUI Environment or @Observable store so all ride-screen views react to theme changes.
- SolarScheduleService: shared utility computing local sunrise/sunset from device coordinates + timezone; used by both BrightnessController and ThemeAutoSwitcher.
- ThemeAutoSwitcher: when auto-theme enabled, listens to solar schedule and sets active theme to Night at sunset, Day at sunrise. Amber is manual-only (not part of auto rotation). Publishes active theme to DisplayTheme store.
- BrightnessController: publishes a 0.0–1.0 brightness multiplier. Primary path: sample UIScreen.main.brightness periodically. Fallback path: solar schedule dim at sunset / brighten at sunrise. Supports manual override flag.
- RideView: main speedometer UI with stat tiles; reads DisplayTheme + BrightnessController.
- SettingsView: unit toggle, display theme picker (Day / Night / Amber), auto-theme toggle, auto-brightness toggle + manual slider, odometer reset (with confirmation), permissions status.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
Phase 1 — Foundation
- Task 1.1: Xcode project scaffold (SwiftUI, iOS 16+, bundle ID, location usage strings) (traces: NFR-3)
- Task 1.2: TripComputerEngine with unit tests for speed conversion, averaging, max tracking, jitter filter (traces: FR-8, NFR-4, NFR-5)
- Task 1.3: TripStore persistence for odometer, unit preference, display theme, auto-theme flag, brightness settings (traces: FR-4, FR-5, FR-10, FR-12, FR-14)
- Task 1.4: DisplayTheme model + Day/Night/Amber palettes with unit tests for palette completeness (traces: FR-9, NFR-7, NFR-8)
- Task 1.5: SolarScheduleService — sunrise/sunset calc from lat/lon/timezone with unit tests (traces: FR-11, FR-13)

Phase 2 — Location & UI
- Task 2.1: LocationService with permission flow and graceful denial state (traces: FR-1, FR-6)
- Task 2.2: RideView — large speed display + stat tiles; theme-aware colors via DisplayTheme environment (traces: FR-1, FR-2, FR-3, FR-4, FR-9, NFR-1)
- Task 2.3: Reset Trip button + odometer reset in Settings with confirmation (traces: FR-2, FR-4)

Phase 3 — Display adaptability
- Task 3.1: BrightnessController — ambient-light sampling with solar-schedule fallback; smooth 1 Hz throttle (traces: FR-11, NFR-6)
- Task 3.2: ThemeAutoSwitcher — Night at sunset, Day at sunrise when auto-theme enabled; Amber remains manual-only (traces: FR-13, FR-14)
- Task 3.3: SettingsView — theme picker (Day / Night / Amber), auto-theme toggle, auto-brightness toggle, manual brightness slider (traces: FR-9, FR-10, FR-12, FR-14)
- Task 3.4: Ride Mode — keep screen awake while trip active (traces: FR-7)
- Task 3.5: SettingsView — unit picker, permission status link to Settings (traces: FR-5)

Phase 4 — Polish
- Task 4.1: XCTest coverage pass to meet 85% threshold (traces: NFR-5)
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit tests for TripComputerEngine (speed math, averaging, max tracking, jitter filter, unit conversion). Unit tests for DisplayTheme (Night palette is all-red-channel, Amber palette is amber-channel only, Day palette meets contrast targets). Unit tests for SolarScheduleService (known lat/lon/date produces correct sunrise/sunset). Unit tests for ThemeAutoSwitcher (switches to Night after sunset, Day after sunrise; disabled when auto-theme off; Amber selection pins theme and disables auto rotation). Unit tests for BrightnessController (solar fallback returns dim at night / bright at day; manual override disables auto; throttle limits update rate). LocationService tested via protocol injection with mock locations. UI smoke tests for permission-denied, active-ride, theme-switch, auto-theme toggle, and brightness-override states. Manual device testing on a bicycle/motorcycle ride for GPS accuracy and theme legibility (Day, Night, Amber) validation.
narratives.Deployment: Xcode build → TestFlight for beta → App Store. Location permission strings required in Info.plist. No backend or secrets for MVP.
narratives.OpenQuestions:
1. Analog speedometer dial vs digital-only display — defaulting to digital for MVP readability.
2. Background location updates — deferred; When-In-Use is sufficient for MVP with screen-awake Ride Mode.
3. Trip history log — deferred post-MVP.
4. Additional color themes beyond Day/Night/Amber (e.g. high-vis green) — deferred post-MVP; architecture supports adding presets.
5. Ambient-light primary path vs solar-only fallback for brightness — implement both; prefer ambient sampling, fall back to solar schedule when brightness API is unavailable or user enables 'schedule only' mode.
6. Amber in auto-theme rotation — Amber is manual-only for MVP; auto-theme toggles only between Day (sunrise) and Night (sunset).

narrative.Acceptance: SwiftUI iOS 16+ app builds and runs in simulator with location usage description in Info.plist.
Pure Swift engine computes speed, max, avg, distance, jitter filter; ≥ 85% coverage on engine module.
Requests When-In-Use permission; publishes speed updates; shows helpful UI when denied.
Large speed display plus trip distance, max speed, avg speed, and odometer visible at a glance.
User can select Day, Night (all-red-on-black), or Amber (warm amber-on-dark) theme; selection applies immediately and persists across launches.
When auto-theme enabled (default on): switches to Night at local sunset, Day at local sunrise. User can disable auto-theme or pin Amber manually.
Brightness dims in low light (ambient sampling) and at sunset; brightens in daylight and at sunrise; manual slider override available; updates throttled smoothly.
User can switch mph/km/h, pick display theme, control brightness; Ride Mode keeps screen awake; trip and odometer reset work with confirmation.
